description: Classification golden set for Engram memory kind/wing/room classification. Each sample is a realistic memory write with expected classification. Used to measure rule-based and LLM classification accuracy.

samples.content: User prefers dark mode and never wants email notifications.
Never store API keys, passwords, or credentials in memory items. The safety module checks before every write.
samples.notes: Security rule that must always hold

samples.content: We decided to use PostgreSQL with pgvector for all storage instead of Redis.
The production database is running on engram01 at 10.0.0.22 port 5432.
All PRs must pass CI before merge. No exceptions, even for trivial docs changes.
The API response time seemed slow today around 3pm, maybe 2-3 seconds per request.
To deploy Engram: docker compose up -d, then run engram bootstrap-key to get the first API key.
Session summary: we closed the RLS isolation bug, shipped the trust-state writer fixes, and upgraded the dogfood database with zero data loss.
User prefers concise responses without excessive hedging or disclaimers.

The remember endpoint validates content_hash using SHA-256 and enforces dedup via a partial unique index on (tenant_id, workspace_id, principal_id, content_hash) WHERE valid_to IS NULL AND review_status != 'rejected'.
samples.notes: Technical implementation detail — a fact, not a procedure

samples.content: Content is append-first: never UPDATE memory item content. Metadata changes go through PATCH which writes to item_events first, then updates the column.
samples.notes: Architectural rule governing how code must behave

samples.content: The meeting went well — stakeholders agreed to prioritize the trust model over the UI for the first release.
We must never bypass Row Level Security. RLS is enforced at the database level on all tenant-scoped tables. Application-level WHERE clauses are defense-in-depth, not a substitute.
Agent-sourced manual writes default to review_status='proposed' at 0.6 trust, preventing agents from getting user-level trust automatically.
User wants low-downtime migrations and prefers consolidating idle power-hungry hosts to save electricity.

To upgrade the dogfood database: back up first with pg_dump, start a temporary pgvector container on the data volume, apply migrations 003-013 in sequence via psql, verify table counts and RLS, then baseline the migration tracker.
The service appeared to work but semantic search returned empty results because the embedding worker was failing silently with a 401 authentication error.

Startup recall is deterministic given state: same corpus, item states, and config produce the same output. It is NOT deterministic across time because recall mutates last_recalled_at and recall_count.
samples.notes: Design contract — a fact about the system

samples.content: All trust defaults and scoring weights are stored in tenant_config, not hardcoded. The remember and recall endpoints must read the active tenant_config to get current values.
We chose FastAPI over Flask because async support and Pydantic v2 validation were critical for the trust model's request validation needs.
